citations.text: Brinkmann, L., Baumann, F., Bonnefon, J. F., Derex, M., Müller, T. F., Nussberger, A. M., Czaplicka, A., Acerbi, A., Griffiths, T. L., Henrich, J., Leibo, J. Z., McElreath, R., Oudeyer, P. Y., Stray, J., & Rahwan, I. (2023). Machine culture. Nature Human Behaviour, 7(11), 1855-1868.

en.general: Culture that is mediated or generated by machines — cases where AI systems shape, participate in, or themselves undergo the variation, transmission, and selection dynamics that define cultural evolution, rather than machines being only passive tools culture is transmitted through.

examples: A recommendation algorithm's outputs shifting collective media consumption patterns, which in turn shifts what content gets produced and fed back into the algorithm's training data.
